skill.name: code-documentation-doc-generate
skill.description: Generate maintainable API, architecture, user, and developer documentation from existing code and project context.
skill.summary: Creates structured documentation plans, templates, diagrams, and automation guidance from codebases.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, environment, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are documentation examples, placeholders, or markdown references, with no executable skill code, prompt injection, or confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Command Patterns Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur inside markdown code fences, resource links, or CI examples. They illustrate documentation workflows and are not executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are in markdown examples and templates. No file defines a runnable script, install hook, or hidden command path.

low_findings.title: Network References Are Placeholder Documentation Content
low_findings.description: The URL, fetch, and HTTP library detections appear in OpenAPI, Swagger UI, and generated API client examples. They use example domains or CDN references for documentation templates.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network examples are visible documentation samples, not covert outbound requests. The skill has no executable network client outside the examples.

low_findings.title: Credential Terms Are Placeholders And Safety Guidance
low_findings.description: The environment and secret detections are placeholder documentation fields, example Authorization headers, and GitHub Actions token references. The skill also warns against exposing secrets in generated docs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited credential strings are placeholders or standard CI syntax. I found no evidence that real secrets are collected, requested, or transmitted.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access Is Expected Codebase Analysis
low_findings.description: The filesystem pattern appears in code samples that parse source files to measure documentation coverage. This matches the documented purpose of generating docs from a codebase.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reading source files is integral to documentation generation. There is no evidence of broad hidden harvesting or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Alerts Are Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The cited locations contain documentation prose, markdown templates, schema examples, or diagram syntax. No weak cryptographic operation or system reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection of these lines did not show crypto APIs, hashing calls, host enumeration, or shell reconnaissance. The matches are caused by benign documentation text and formatting.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable Workflow Examples In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The playbook includes shell and CI examples that an assistant might adapt into a project. They are not malicious, but users should review generated automation before running it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are legitimate, but generated automation can affect a repository if copied into CI or scripts. Human review is appropriate before execution.

content.value_statement: Codebases often lack current API, architecture, and user documentation. This skill turns source code and project context into structured documentation plans, templates, and automation guidance.
actual_capabilities: Plans API, architecture, code, user, and developer documentation from repository context.
Suggests OpenAPI, Swagger UI, Redoc, Mermaid, PlantUML, and README structures.
Provides templates for component docs, user guides, and troubleshooting references.
Outlines documentation CI workflows, linting, coverage checks, and static site publishing.
Identifies missing docstrings and documentation gaps using source analysis patterns.
Includes safety guidance to avoid exposing secrets or sensitive internal details.
limitations: Requires access to source code or project requirements to produce accurate documentation.
Does not verify runtime API behavior unless tests, schemas, or live specs are provided.
Generated CI and command examples need review before use in a real repository.
May need project-specific editing for product language, compliance, and audience tone.

use_cases.title: Create API Reference Docs
use_cases.description: Generate endpoint summaries, schemas, authentication notes, and interactive API documentation structure from backend code.

use_cases.title: Document System Architecture
use_cases.description: Produce component descriptions, Mermaid diagrams, dependency notes, and operational context for service-based systems.

use_cases.title: Standardize Project Documentation
use_cases.description: Create README, setup, contribution, troubleshooting, and user guide templates across a repository.

prompt_templates.title: Generate A Documentation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a documentation roadmap.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repository and create a documentation plan. Include API docs, README updates, architecture diagrams, and user guides. List assumptions and missing source information.

prompt_templates.title: Create API Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for backend API docs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the API routes, request models, response models, and authentication flow. Draft an OpenAPI-oriented documentation outline with endpoint descriptions and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Build Architecture Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Create architecture documentation for this codebase. Include component responsibilities, service dependencies, data flow diagrams, deployment notes, and reliability considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Design Documentation Automation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for maintainable documentation pipelines.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a documentation automation workflow for this repository. Include generation steps, linting, coverage checks, CI triggers, publishing, and review gates for generated content.

output_examples.input: A FastAPI service with route handlers, Pydantic models, and sparse README content.
output: A documentation plan covering endpoint reference, model descriptions, authentication notes, and setup instructions.
An OpenAPI documentation outline with sections for parameters, responses, errors, and examples.
A list of missing source details that require human confirmation.

output_examples.input: A microservices repository with frontend, gateway, services, database, cache, and storage components.
output: Architecture documentation with component responsibilities and service dependency notes.
A diagram plan for frontend, gateway, service, and data-layer relationships.
Operational documentation topics for scaling, reliability, and troubleshooting.

output_examples.input: A library project that needs contributor docs and documentation quality checks.
output: README and contribution guide structure with installation, testing, and release sections.
Documentation coverage checklist for modules, functions, classes, and examples.
CI workflow guidance for generating, validating, and publishing documentation.

best_practices: Provide the current source code, public API contracts, and target audience before generating docs.
Review generated examples for secrets, internal URLs, and inaccurate operational details.
Keep generated documentation tied to tests, schemas, or CI checks when possible.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated docs without verifying them against the current codebase.
Do not include real credentials, private endpoints, or sensitive architecture details in examples.
Do not add generated CI workflows to production repositories without human review.

faq.question: What documentation can this skill generate?
faq.answer: It can help create API references, architecture docs, README files, user guides, developer guides, and documentation automation plans.

faq.question: Does it need access to my code?
faq.answer: Yes. Accurate documentation requires source files, schemas, configuration, comments, or clear requirements as a source of truth.

faq.question: Can it create diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft Mermaid or PlantUML diagram structures for components, services, and data flows.

faq.question: Can it generate OpenAPI documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline OpenAPI specs and interactive documentation structures when route and schema information is available.

faq.question: Is generated documentation safe to publish?
faq.answer: Review it first. Remove secrets, private URLs, internal-only details, and inaccurate claims before publishing.

faq.question: Does it automate documentation updates?
faq.answer: It can design CI and validation workflows, but generated commands should be reviewed before use.
